Name:
🏆 Teen Wolf
(prod. Ratatat)
(Alive)
Notes: Confirmed on Twitter by Kid Cudi to the title of a song worked on around June 2009. Likely an alt title for Alive (Nightmare) on Man On The Moon 1, due to the lyrics "Every time, the moon shines I become alive, yeah" resembling the habits of a werewolf  transforming during full moons. Likely inspired by the 1985 movie "Teen Wolf" or the 1986 TV series "Teen Wolf." This was spoken about in a 2009 interview where Cudi discusses and compares the song to becoming a werewolf, meaning "Teen Wolf" was likely an old name before "Alive" was the title.

Likely JAY-Z never recorded on it.

Name: A Man Named Scott
Notes:
(01/26/2011) (Kid Cudi announces the mixtape on Twitter)
(08/21/2011) (Kid Cudi tweets the mixtape is scrapped)
(2011)
Creation Date: In January of 2011, Cudi would announce a mixtape on Twitter that was to serve as a sequel to 'A Kid Named Cudi' named 'A Man Named Scott.' The mixtape was created as a sort of companion piece to 'WZRD'. the rock album collaboration album with producer Dot Da Genius, as Cudi expressed that Wizard wouldn't be rap focused at all, and that fans of his rapping should stay tuned for 'A Man Named Scott' instead. In August of 2011, Cudi would again take to Twitter to announce that the mixtape had been cancelled in order to focus on 'WZRD' and 'Man On The Moon III'.

(2011-2012)
Creation Date: "WZRD" is the debut studio album by American alternative rock duo WZRD, composed of musicians Dot da Genius and Kid Cudi. It was released on February 28, 2012, via Universal Republic Records and GOOD Music, as well as Cudi's newly found label imprint, Wicked Awesome Records. The album was supported by the singles "Brake" and "Teleport 2 Me, Jamie." For the project Cudi went sober from everything and used it as inspiration for the album.

Name: INDICUD
Notes:
(06/05/2012) (Kid Cudi announces 'INDICUD' on Twitter, dedicated to Ben Breedlove)
(04/16/2013) ('INDICUD' officially releases)
(2012-2013)
Creation Date: "INDICUD" is the third studio album by American rapper and singer Kid Cudi, released on April 12, 2013. The album marked a departure from Cudi's earlier style, as he took on a more prominent role in production and instrumentation. The album features a mix of introspective, personal lyrics, and a darker, more experimental sound, and includes guest appearances from artists such as Kendrick Lamar, A$AP Rocky, and Michael Bolton. 'Indicud' was well-received by fans and critics alike, with many praising Cudi's growth as an artist and the album's innovative sound. The lead single from the album, "Just What I Am," was a commercial success and helped to solidify Cudi's position as a unique and influential voice in hip-hop and alternative music.

Creation Date: At a concert in Dallas is the first mention of the project, then announced on the 27th of October, 2013, by DatNewCudi on Twitter. 'Satellite Flight: The Journey to Mother Moon' is the fourth studio album by American rapper and singer Kid Cudi. It was released on February 25, 2014, and was originally intended to be an EP, but was later expanded into a full-length album. The album features a more experimental sound, with a greater emphasis on live instrumentation and a more cohesive, concept-driven narrative. The album received generally mixed reviews, with many praising its introspective, atmospheric sound and Cudi's continued evolution as an artist. Despite its critical acclaim, 'Satellite Flight: The Journey to Mother Moon' performed modestly commercially, but remains a fan favorite and a highlight in Kid Cudi's discography.

Notes:

(2015-2016)
Creation Date: On 05/11/2016, Kid Cudi addressed "Man On The Moon III" rumors and revealed 'PP&DS.' On October 5th, 2016, Cudi would check into rehab for depression and suicidal urges. The album features a mix of introspective and introverted lyrics, which showcase Kid Cudi's personal struggles and journey towards self-discovery. The album draws inspiration from various genres including alternative, hip-hop, and rock, and features guest appearances from Travis Scott, Pharrell Williams, and Willow Smith. The album received positive reviews from fans and critics, who praised its raw and emotional themes, as well as Kid Cudi's unique and authentic approach to music. 'Passion, Pain, & Demon Slayin'' is considered one of Kid Cudi's best works and is a must-listen for fans of alternative hip-hop.

Creation Date: Out of the five Wyoming projects from 2018, people consider "KIDS SEE GHOSTS" to be one of the best. This album focuses heavily on overcoming struggles caused by mental health, which Kanye and Cudi had been fighting. It is characterized by many psychedelic and rock-influenced elements, making for an album that sounds like nothing else. This album was officially released on June 8th, 2018, making it Kanye's second collab album.

Name: Man On The Moon III: The Chosen
Notes:
(??/??/2019) (Kid Cudi makes "2 or 3" songs that don't fit the current projects, he turns them into 'Man On The Moon III: The Chosen') 
(12/11/2020) ('Man On The Moon III: The Chosen' officially releases)
(2019-2020)
Creation Date: "Man on the Moon III: The Chosen" is the (alleged) final installment in Kid Cudi's highly acclaimed 'Man on the Moon' trilogy. The album showcases the rapper and singer's versatility and range as an artist, blending elements of hip-hop, R&B, and alternative rock to create an emotional and thought-provoking musical journey. The album features introspective lyrics that explore themes of self-discovery, growth, and resilience. This would be worked on at the same time as 'THE SCOTTS' album, which would later be confirmed canceled in late 2022, then in April 2023 Cudi stated "maybe" to the idea of the album again.

(2022)
Creation Date: "Entergalactic" is the eighth studio album by American rapper and singer Kid Cudi, released on September 30, 2022. The album is the soundtrack to the Netflix animated series of the same name, which was created and written by Kid Cudi himself. 'Entergalactic' features a mix of hip-hop, psychedelic rock, and electronic music, and showcases Cudi's versatility as an artist. The album's concept revolves around a fantasy version of Scott's life and is centered around themes of love, adventure, and sorrow. Meant to feel like "an adventure around a city and a new chapter of life."

Creation Date: In late 2022, Kid Cudi announced his retirement, with one last album to finish it off. Eventually in February 2023, Cudi stated he was no longer retiring and there was more to come. It is speculated to build on multiple 2021 tracks. The first single was released June 2nd, 2023, while the full album will release September 22nd, 2023 and is a collaborative effort with DJ Drama. Named after a scene from the movie "Waterboy."

Track Length: On May 15th, 2023, a tagged copy of Kid Cudi's upcoming album "INSANO" leaked online. This caused several delays and threw Kid Cudi completely off. Songs were removed and reworked for the final release. The track ordering is unknown.

Name: FREE
Notes:
(2/23/2024) ("INSANO (NITRO MEGA)" is released)
(5/9/2025) (Kid Cudi releases "Neverland" in May)
(8/22/2025) (Kid Cudi releases "FREE")
Creation Date: After the release of "INSANO (NITRO MEGA)," Kid Cudi announced on Twitter that his next album was already underway. By August 2024, he confirmed the album was already complete, and he would rather focus on his comic book and acting career for the time being until he drops it. Cudi would then release the single "Neverland" on May 9th, 2025, also announcing the album's title "FREE," stating that it would be Pop-Oriented. Cudi then released FREE on August 22nd, 2025. It's worth mentioning that King Chip teased "The Almighty GloryUs" in October 2024, potentially meaning the scrapped collaboration album between the two could release at some point. Kid Cudi also teased a potential fourth entry in the Man On The Moon series in an anniversary post for Man On The Moon II.
